2509.07174.studies_paper_title: Erdős--Pósa property of cycles that are far apart
2509.07174.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full generality with explicit bounds $f(k)\in\mathcal{O}(k^{18}\operatorname{polylog}k)$ and $g(d)=19d$.
2509.07174.attributed_to: Chudnovsky and Seymour; also Ahn, Gollin, Huynh, and Kwon

2508.14332.studies_paper_title: Erdős--Pósa property of cycles that are far apart
2508.14332.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full generality with explicit bounds $f(k)\in\mathcal{O}(k^{18}\operatorname{polylog}k)$ and $g(d)=19d$.
2508.14332.attributed_to: Chudnovsky and Seymour; also Ahn, Gollin, Huynh, and Kwon

2403.08303.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the Erdős-Hajnal property
2403.08303.paper_contribution: The paper's results establishing the Erdős-Hajnal property for new (infinitely many prime) graph classes implicitly verify Conjecture 1.7 for those classes, via the equivalence proved by Bucić, Fox, and Pham.

2404.02021.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension
2404.02021.paper_contribution: Fully confirms the conjecture, proving that every graph $G$ of VC-dimension at most $d$ contains a clique or stable set of size at least $|G|^{c_d}$ for an explicit $c_d>0$ satisfying $c_d \geq 2^{-2^{O(d)}}$.

2402.08418.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension
2402.08418.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for every two-colourable tournament, which in particular completes the verification of the conjecture for all six-vertex tournaments.

2402.08418.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VII. The five-vertex path
2402.08418.paper_contribution: Proves that Conjecture 1.4 holds for $H = P_5$ (Theorem 1.5), which in particular implies the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture for $P_5$.

2302.04986.paper_contribution: Provides a second, simpler counterexample to Conjecture 1.5 that additionally has arbitrarily large girth and does not contain all trees as induced subgraphs; the proof establishes a slightly stronger property than (v) — see Theorem 6.2.

2304.03567.paper_contribution: Obtains improved upper and lower bounds: $0.555197\dots \leq p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^{2}) \leq 0.8457$.
Significantly improves the best known upper bound, establishing $\lim_{n\to\infty}p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^{n})\leq 0.5847$.

2307.08361.studies_paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
2307.08361.paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by constructing, for all integers $\ell, m \geq 1$, a graph $G$ and sets $S, T \subseteq V(G)$ witnessing its failure when $c = k = 3$ (Theorem 1.3); a variant of the counterexample has only one vertex of degree more than three.

2201.04062.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal
2201.04062.paper_contribution: Proves that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\kappa(G)\geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}\,\log\log|G|}$ (Theorem 1.3), the first improvement over the $2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}}$ bound for general $H$ since 1977, obtained as a corollary of a stronger quantitative result (Theorem 1.8) that also strengthens theorems of Rödl, Nikiforov, and Fox–Sudakov.

2201.04062.studies_paper_title: Some results and problems on tournament structure
2201.04062.paper_contribution: Proves two partial results in its favour: Theorem 1.3 establishes the case where $A$ is a cyclic triangle (and $\chi(B)\geq c$), and Theorem 1.4 establishes the conjecture for all tournaments with sufficiently large domination number.

2309.07905.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the Erdős-Hajnal property
2309.07905.paper_contribution: Proves infinitely many prime graphs satisfy the conjecture: all graphs $H$ whose every non-trivial prime induced subgraph $H'$ has a vertex of degree one and a vertex of degree $|H'|-2$; more generally, proves it whenever $H_1$ and $\overline{H_2}$ are both buildable (every prime induced subgraph with at least three vertices has a vertex of degree one).

2305.13422.studies_paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
2305.13422.paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by constructing, for all integers $\ell, m \geq 1$, a graph $G$ and sets $S, T \subseteq V(G)$ witnessing its failure when $c = k = 3$ (Theorem 1.3); a variant of the counterexample has only one vertex of degree more than three.

2202.05557.paper_contribution: Proves that every $\{H,K_{k+1}\}$-free graph $G$ with $H$ a forest satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq |G|^{1-o(1)}$ (Theorem 1.2/1.3/1.4); proves the full linear stable-set conclusion of the conjecture for all 'multibrooms' $H$; and deduces a fractional-colouring version of the conjecture for all multibrooms.

Proves a polylogarithmic analogue for $K_t$-subdivision-free graphs: every such graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq c_t|G|/(\log|G|)^{3t-5}$, showing that the Gyárfás-Sumner phenomenon (large stable sets in $\chi$-bounded classes) extends — in a near sense — well beyond the forest-exclusion setting.

Proves that every $\{H,K_{k+1}\}$-free graph $G$ with $H$ a forest satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq |G|^{1-o(1)}$ (Theorem 1.2/1.3/1.4); proves the full linear stable-set conclusion of the conjecture for all 'multibrooms' $H$; and deduces a fractional-colouring version of the conjecture for all multibrooms.

Proves a polylogarithmic analogue for $K_t$-subdivision-free graphs: every such graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq c_t|G|/(\log|G|)^{3t-5}$, showing that the Gyárfás-Sumner phenomenon (large stable sets in $\chi$-bounded classes) extends — in a near sense — well beyond the forest-exclusion setting.

2202.05557.studies_paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
2202.05557.paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by constructing, for all integers $\ell, m \geq 1$, a graph $G$ and sets $S, T \subseteq V(G)$ witnessing its failure when $c = k = 3$ (Theorem 1.3); a variant of the counterexample has only one vertex of degree more than three.

2210.16971.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VII. The five-vertex path
2210.16971.paper_contribution: Proves that Conjecture 1.4 holds for $H = P_5$ (Theorem 1.5), which in particular implies the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture for $P_5$.

2210.16971.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VII. The five-vertex path
2210.16971.paper_contribution: Proves that Conjecture 1.4 holds for $H = P_5$ (Theorem 1.5), which in particular implies the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture for $P_5$.

2203.06775.paper_contribution: Provides a second, simpler counterexample to Conjecture 1.5 that additionally has arbitrarily large girth and does not contain all trees as induced subgraphs; the proof establishes a slightly stronger property than (v) — see Theorem 6.2.

2203.06775.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XV. Even-hole-free graphs with bounded clique number have logarithmic treewidth
2203.06775.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full: for every integer $t\geq 1$ there exists $c_t\geq 1$ such that every $n$-vertex even-hole-free graph with no clique of size $t$ has $\operatorname{tw}(G)\leq c_t\log n$.

2203.06775.studies_paper_title: Asymptotic structure. V. The coarse Menger conjecture in bounded path-width
2203.06775.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for all graphs of bounded path-width (and, further, for graphs of bounded coarse path-width); also notes the conjecture remains false for bounded tree-width.
2203.06775.attributed_to: Albrechtsen, Huynh, Jacobs, Knappe, and Wollan; and independently Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu

2203.06775.studies_paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
2203.06775.paper_contribution: The authors established in their prior work [9] that this conjecture is false for all $k \geq 3$, even when $c = 3$ and $G$ has maximum degree three; the present paper then addresses the weaker Conjecture 1.2.
2203.06775.attributed_to: Albrechtsen, Huynh, Jacobs, Knappe, and Wollan; and independently Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu

2203.06775.studies_paper_title: Erdős--Pósa property of cycles that are far apart
2203.06775.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full generality with explicit bounds $f(k)\in\mathcal{O}(k^{18}\operatorname{polylog}k)$ and $g(d)=19d$.
2203.06775.attributed_to: Chudnovsky and Seymour; also Ahn, Gollin, Huynh, and Kwon

2110.00278.paper_contribution: Proves that every $\{H,K_{k+1}\}$-free graph $G$ with $H$ a forest satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq |G|^{1-o(1)}$ (Theorem 1.2/1.3/1.4); proves the full linear stable-set conclusion of the conjecture for all 'multibrooms' $H$; and deduces a fractional-colouring version of the conjecture for all multibrooms.

Proves a polylogarithmic analogue for $K_t$-subdivision-free graphs: every such graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq c_t|G|/(\log|G|)^{3t-5}$, showing that the Gyárfás-Sumner phenomenon (large stable sets in $\chi$-bounded classes) extends — in a near sense — well beyond the forest-exclusion setting.

2110.00278.studies_paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. V. Excluding a tree of radius two and a complete multipartite graph
2110.00278.paper_contribution: Proves that for every tree $H$ of radius two and every integer $d \geq 1$, there is a polynomial $f$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f(\tau_d(G))$ for every $H$-free graph $G$ (Theorem 1.6), which in particular yields a doubly-exponential bound on $\chi(G)$ in terms of $\omega(G)$ for $H$-free graphs when $H$ is a tree of radius two.

2110.00278.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal
2110.00278.paper_contribution: Proves that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\kappa(G)\geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}\,\log\log|G|}$ (Theorem 1.3), the first improvement over the $2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}}$ bound for general $H$ since 1977, obtained as a corollary of a stronger quantitative result (Theorem 1.8) that also strengthens theorems of Rödl, Nikiforov, and Fox–Sudakov.

2110.00278.studies_paper_title: Pure pairs. IV. Trees in bipartite graphs
2110.00278.paper_contribution: Proves bipartite analogues of the strong Erdős-Hajnal property (Theorems 1.5 and 1.6), establishing that excluding a forest bigraph and its bicomplement forces linear-sized pure pairs in each part, and providing one-sided sparse versions; these results imply bipartite Erdős-Hajnal-type bounds.

2110.00278.studies_paper_title: Pure pairs. IV. Trees in bipartite graphs
2110.00278.paper_contribution: No progress is made on this conjecture in the paper; it is highlighted as an important open problem that goes beyond the main results.

2110.00278.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. II. Sparse and dense sets in cographs
2110.00278.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $H=P_4$ with the optimal $\delta=\varepsilon$ (and a stronger maximum-degree bound), and more generally for all graphs $H$ obtainable by vertex-substitution from copies of $P_4$ and its subgraphs, via the stronger 'viral' property.
Disproves the conjecture for all $k\geqslant 3$ by constructing $G$ and $T$ such that the out-neighbourhood of any set of at most $\frac{\log|V(T)|}{2\chi^2}$ vertices is bipartite.

2110.00278.studies_paper_title: Erdős--Pósa property of cycles that are far apart
2110.00278.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full generality with explicit bounds $f(k)\in\mathcal{O}(k^{18}\operatorname{polylog}k)$ and $g(d)=19d$.
2110.00278.attributed_to: Chudnovsky and Seymour; also Ahn, Gollin, Huynh, and Kwon

2110.00278.studies_paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
2110.00278.paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by constructing, for all integers $\ell, m \geq 1$, a graph $G$ and sets $S, T \subseteq V(G)$ witnessing its failure when $c = k = 3$ (Theorem 1.3); a variant of the counterexample has only one vertex of degree more than three.

2202.09118.studies_paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number VI. Adding a four-vertex path
2202.09118.paper_contribution: Proves that if $H$ is a good forest then the disjoint union of $H$ and $P_4$ is also good (polynomially $\chi$-bounded), in particular showing that $2P_4$-free graphs satisfy $\chi(G) \leq \omega(G)^{16}$; more generally proves polynomial $\chi$-boundedness for $\{H_1, H_2\}$-free graphs when every component of $H_1$ is good and $H_2$ is a broom or disjoint union of a good forest and paths.

2202.09118.studies_paper_title: Suns in triangle-free graphs of large chromatic number
2202.09118.paper_contribution: Derives a partial answer analogous to their main Theorem 1.3 by combining it with Hajebi's result on bull-free graphs (Theorem 1.5), extending the chromatic-boundedness conclusion to the net-free sun-free setting under analogous exclusion conditions.

2202.09118.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XIV. Non-adjacent neighbours in a hole
2202.09118.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full (Theorem 1.1): every clock-free graph of large treewidth contains, as an induced subgraph, one of the basic obstructions — a complete graph, a subdivision of a wall, or the line graph of a subdivision of a wall.
2202.09118.attributed_to: Abrishami, Alecu, Chudnovsky, Hajebi, Spirkl, and Vušković

2202.09118.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XIV. Non-adjacent neighbours in a hole
2202.09118.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full (Theorem 1.1): every clock-free graph of large treewidth contains, as an induced subgraph, one of the basic obstructions — a complete graph, a subdivision of a wall, or the line graph of a subdivision of a wall.
2202.09118.attributed_to: Abrishami, Alecu, Chudnovsky, Hajebi, Spirkl, and Vušković

2202.09118.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XIV. Non-adjacent neighbours in a hole
2202.09118.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full (Theorem 1.1): every clock-free graph of large treewidth contains, as an induced subgraph, one of the basic obstructions — a complete graph, a subdivision of a wall, or the line graph of a subdivision of a wall.
2202.09118.attributed_to: Abrishami, Alecu, Chudnovsky, Hajebi, Spirkl, and Vušković

2103.05036.studies_paper_title: Expected number of faces in a random embedding of any graph is at most linear
2103.05036.paper_contribution: Proved as a corollary of Theorem 3, which establishes $E[F] \leq n(H_{2\mu}+1)$; for simple graphs this specialises to the linear bound, further sharpened to $E[F] \leq \frac{\pi^2}{6}n$ by Theorem 8.
2103.05036.attributed_to: Halasz, Masarik, Samal, Campion Loth, and Mohar

2103.05036.studies_paper_title: Expected number of faces in a random embedding of any graph is at most linear
2103.05036.paper_contribution: Proved as a corollary of Theorem 3: $E[F] \leq n(H_{2\mu}+1)$, and since $H_{2\mu} \sim \log(2\mu)+\gamma$, this gives $O(n\log\mu)$; the bound is tight up to a constant factor.
2103.05036.attributed_to: Halasz, Masarik, Samal, Campion Loth, and Mohar

2103.05036.studies_paper_title: Random Embeddings of Graphs: The Expected Number of Faces in Most Graphs is Logarithmic
2103.05036.paper_contribution: Proves (Theorem 1.8) that $\mathbb{E}[F(K_n)] = \Theta(\ln n)$, confirming the logarithmic order; the exact constant $2\ln n + O(1)$ remains open.

2108.00987.studies_paper_title: Pure pairs. V. Excluding some long subdivision
2108.00987.paper_contribution: Proves this conjecture in full; specifically establishes (1.6) that for all $c > 0$ and sufficiently large $n$, every $n$-vertex perfect graph $G$ admits a pure pair $A, B$ with $|A|, |B| \geq n^{1-c}$, and further strengthens it (1.7–1.9) by making one side linear, relaxing the perfect graph hypothesis to excluding a single long hole and a single long antihole, and ultimately excluding any graph of sufficiently large branch-length and its complement.

2004.07457.paper_contribution: Proves that every bipartite graph $G$ of sufficiently large maximum degree $\Delta$ satisfies $\operatorname{ch}(G)<(\frac{4}{5}-\varepsilon)\frac{\Delta}{\log\Delta}$ for $\varepsilon=10^{-3}$, giving the first upper bound with leading constant strictly less than $1$ and showing that list coloring behaves fundamentally differently for bipartite graphs than for general triangle-free graphs.

2108.02685.paper_contribution: The paper does not resolve Conjecture 1.1; it confirms the weaker asymptotic version (Conjecture 1.2) for $d = o(n/(\log n)^{12})$ via the irregular random subgraph model.

Confirms Conjecture 1.2 for all $d = o(n/(\log n)^{12})$ by proving (Theorem 1.3) that the irregular random subgraph $H = H(G)$ satisfies $m(H,k) = (1+o(1))n/(d+1)$ for all $0 \leq k \leq d$ with high probability, via a martingale Bernstein inequality argument.
The paper does not resolve Conjecture 1.1; it confirms the weaker asymptotic version (Conjecture 1.2) for $d = o(n/(\log n)^{12})$ via the irregular random subgraph model.

Confirms Conjecture 1.2 for all $d = o(n/(\log n)^{12})$ by proving (Theorem 1.3) that the irregular random subgraph $H = H(G)$ satisfies $m(H,k) = (1+o(1))n/(d+1)$ for all $0 \leq k \leq d$ with high probability, via a martingale Bernstein inequality argument.

1609.00314.studies_paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. IV. A near-polynomial bound for excluding the five-vertex path
1609.00314.paper_contribution: Proves the near-polynomial bound $\chi(G) \leq \omega(G)^{\log_2(\omega(G))}$ for $P_5$-free graphs with $\omega(G) \geq 3$, making progress towards establishing the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture for $P_5$, currently the smallest open case.

1609.00314.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal
1609.00314.paper_contribution: Proves that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\kappa(G)\geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}\,\log\log|G|}$ (Theorem 1.3), the first improvement over the $2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}}$ bound for general $H$ since 1977, obtained as a corollary of a stronger quantitative result (Theorem 1.8) that also strengthens theorems of Rödl, Nikiforov, and Fox–Sudakov.

Proves that every $\{H,K_{k+1}\}$-free graph $G$ with $H$ a forest satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq |G|^{1-o(1)}$ (Theorem 1.2/1.3/1.4); proves the full linear stable-set conclusion of the conjecture for all 'multibrooms' $H$; and deduces a fractional-colouring version of the conjecture for all multibrooms.

Proves a polylogarithmic analogue for $K_t$-subdivision-free graphs: every such graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq c_t|G|/(\log|G|)^{3t-5}$, showing that the Gyárfás-Sumner phenomenon (large stable sets in $\chi$-bounded classes) extends — in a near sense — well beyond the forest-exclusion setting.

1609.00314.studies_paper_title: A counterexample to a conjecture about triangle-free induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number
1609.00314.paper_contribution: Disproves Conjecture 1.1 for all $r \geq 5$ by constructing, for every $n$, a graph $G$ with $\chi(G) \geq n$ and $\omega(G) \leq 3$ such that every induced subgraph $H$ with $\omega(H) \leq 2$ satisfies $\chi(H) \leq 4$; the case $r = 4$ remains open.

2103.10497.studies_paper_title: Pure pairs. IV. Trees in bipartite graphs
2103.10497.paper_contribution: No progress is made on this conjecture in the paper; it is highlighted as an important open problem that goes beyond the main results.

2102.10220.studies_paper_title: Pure pairs. V. Excluding some long subdivision
2102.10220.paper_contribution: Proves this conjecture in full; specifically establishes (1.6) that for all $c > 0$ and sufficiently large $n$, every $n$-vertex perfect graph $G$ admits a pure pair $A, B$ with $|A|, |B| \geq n^{1-c}$, and further strengthens it (1.7–1.9) by making one side linear, relaxing the perfect graph hypothesis to excluding a single long hole and a single long antihole, and ultimately excluding any graph of sufficiently large branch-length and its complement.

1810.00058.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal
1810.00058.paper_contribution: Proves that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\kappa(G)\geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}\,\log\log|G|}$ (Theorem 1.3), the first improvement over the $2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}}$ bound for general $H$ since 1977, obtained as a corollary of a stronger quantitative result (Theorem 1.8) that also strengthens theorems of Rödl, Nikiforov, and Fox–Sudakov.

1810.00058.studies_paper_title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures
1810.00058.paper_contribution: Proves that the polynomial Rödl conjecture is equivalent to the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture; as a corollary deduces that the family of string graphs satisfies the polynomial Rödl conjecture.

1810.00058.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal
1810.00058.paper_contribution: The paper takes a different approach via blockades and leaves this conjecture open, proving the same conclusion (Theorem 1.3) by other means without establishing the conjecture.

1810.00058.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the Erdős-Hajnal property
1810.00058.paper_contribution: The paper's results establishing the Erdős-Hajnal property for new (infinitely many prime) graph classes implicitly verify Conjecture 1.7 for those classes, via the equivalence proved by Bucić, Fox, and Pham.

1810.00058.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. II. Sparse and dense sets in cographs
1810.00058.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $H=P_4$ with the optimal $\delta=\varepsilon$ (and a stronger maximum-degree bound), and more generally for all graphs $H$ obtainable by vertex-substitution from copies of $P_4$ and its subgraphs, via the stronger 'viral' property.

1810.00058.studies_paper_title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures
1810.00058.paper_contribution: Proves that the polynomial Rödl conjecture is equivalent to the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture; as a corollary deduces that the family of string graphs satisfies the polynomial Rödl conjecture.

1810.00058.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the Erdős-Hajnal property
1810.00058.paper_contribution: The paper's results establishing the Erdős-Hajnal property for new (infinitely many prime) graph classes implicitly verify Conjecture 1.7 for those classes, via the equivalence proved by Bucić, Fox, and Pham.

1810.00058.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. II. Sparse and dense sets in cographs
1810.00058.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $H=P_4$ with the optimal $\delta=\varepsilon$ (and a stronger maximum-degree bound), and more generally for all graphs $H$ obtainable by vertex-substitution from copies of $P_4$ and its subgraphs, via the stronger 'viral' property.

2001.01607.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions IV. (Even hole, diamond, pyramid)-free graphs
2001.01607.paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 1.4, that for all $t > 0$ there exists $d_t \geq 0$ such that every (even hole, diamond, pyramid, $K_t$)-free graph has treewidth at most $d_t$, which is a special case of Conjecture 1.5.

2001.01607.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions IV. (Even hole, diamond, pyramid)-free graphs
2001.01607.paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 1.4, that for all $t > 0$ there exists $d_t \geq 0$ such that every (even hole, diamond, pyramid, $K_t$)-free graph has treewidth at most $d_t$, which is a special case of Conjecture 1.5.

2009.13319.studies_paper_title: Decomposing and colouring some locally semicomplete digraphs
2009.13319.paper_contribution: Proves the first open special case $F = S_2^+$, $H = C_3$: every locally out-transitive oriented graph has dichromatic number at most 2 (Theorem 3.2), and in fact proves the stronger Theorem 3.3 used for induction.

2009.13319.studies_paper_title: Decomposing and colouring some locally semicomplete digraphs
2009.13319.paper_contribution: Proves the first open special case $F = S_2^+$, $H = C_3$: every locally out-transitive oriented graph has dichromatic number at most 2 (Theorem 3.2), and in fact proves the stronger Theorem 3.3 used for induction.

2009.13319.studies_paper_title: (P6, triangle)-free digraphs have bounded dichromatic number
2009.13319.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the special case of $\overrightarrow{P}_6$-free digraphs with $\omega(D) \leq 2$ (triangle-free), establishing $\overrightarrow{\chi}(D) \leq 382$ (Theorem 1.3).

2008.05504.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions II. Toward walls and their line graphs in graphs of bounded degree
2008.05504.paper_contribution: Proves three theorems supporting the conjecture: (1) every $S_{t,t,t}$-free graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and sufficiently large treewidth contains the line graph of a subdivision of $W_{k\times k}$ as an induced subgraph (Theorem 1.8); (2) every $(t$-theta, $t$-pyramid)-free graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and sufficiently large treewidth contains the line graph of a subdivision of $W_{k\times k}$ as an induced subgraph (Theorem 1.10); (3) for every subcubic subdivided caterpillar $T$, every graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and sufficiently large treewidth contains a subdivision of $T$ or the line graph of a subdivision of $T$ as an induced subgraph (Theorem 1.11).
2008.05504.attributed_to: Aboulker, Adler, Kim, Sintiari and Trotignon

2008.05504.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions II. Toward walls and their line graphs in graphs of bounded degree
2008.05504.paper_contribution: Proves three theorems supporting the conjecture: (1) every $S_{t,t,t}$-free graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and sufficiently large treewidth contains the line graph of a subdivision of $W_{k\times k}$ as an induced subgraph (Theorem 1.8); (2) every $(t$-theta, $t$-pyramid)-free graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and sufficiently large treewidth contains the line graph of a subdivision of $W_{k\times k}$ as an induced subgraph (Theorem 1.10); (3) for every subcubic subdivided caterpillar $T$, every graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and sufficiently large treewidth contains a subdivision of $T$ or the line graph of a subdivision of $T$ as an induced subgraph (Theorem 1.11).
2008.05504.attributed_to: Aboulker, Adler, Kim, Sintiari and Trotignon

2008.05504.paper_contribution: Provides a second, simpler counterexample to Conjecture 1.5 that additionally has arbitrarily large girth and does not contain all trees as induced subgraphs; the proof establishes a slightly stronger property than (v) — see Theorem 6.2.

2006.09877.studies_paper_title: Twin-width IV: ordered graphs and matrices
2006.09877.paper_contribution: Theorem 1 (i),(iii) resolves the conjecture for hereditary classes of ordered graphs by showing that bounded twin-width and growth $2^{O(n)}$ (i.e., smallness up to isomorphism) are equivalent in this setting.

Disproves the conjecture by constructing (via Theorem 1.1, a finitely generated group with infinite twin-width) a small class of graphs closed under induced subgraphs with unbounded twin-width (Corollary 1.4).

1804.01060.studies_paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
1804.01060.paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by constructing, for all integers $\ell, m \geq 1$, a graph $G$ and sets $S, T \subseteq V(G)$ witnessing its failure when $c = k = 3$ (Theorem 1.3); a variant of the counterexample has only one vertex of degree more than three.

2005.09767.studies_paper_title: A Linear Lower Bound for the Square Energy of Graphs
2005.09767.paper_contribution: Proves the first known linear lower bound $s(G) \geq \frac{3n}{4}$ for every connected graph $G$ of order $n \geq 4$, via a superadditivity result for positive and negative square energies over vertex-induced subgraphs.

1806.00825.studies_paper_title: On Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture
1806.00825.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 1.2 in the case where every color class has size at least $301k \log k$, i.e., when the color class size is $\Omega(k \log k)$.

1806.00825.studies_paper_title: On Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture
1806.00825.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 1.2 in the case where every color class has size at least $301k \log k$, i.e., when the color class size is $\Omega(k \log k)$.

2004.12166.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal
2004.12166.paper_contribution: Proves that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\kappa(G)\geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}\,\log\log|G|}$ (Theorem 1.3), the first improvement over the $2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}}$ bound for general $H$ since 1977, obtained as a corollary of a stronger quantitative result (Theorem 1.8) that also strengthens theorems of Rödl, Nikiforov, and Fox–Sudakov.

1812.07327.studies_paper_title: Fractional chromatic number vs. Hall ratio
1812.07327.paper_contribution: Affirmatively solves the problem by constructing graphs with bounded Hall ratio, arbitrarily large fractional chromatic number, and $\alpha_{\mathrm{deg}_H}(H)\geq |E(H)|/c$ for all subgraphs $H$ (Theorem 1.5).
1812.07327.attributed_to: Dvořák, Ossona de Mendez, and Wu

1812.07327.paper_contribution: Proves that for every polynomial $p$ and integer $a \geq 0$ there exists a polynomial $q$ such that $G$ has a balanced separator which is $(\rho/t)$-cheap with $\exp(q(t))\log^{(a)} n$ outliers (Theorem 3), and with $q(t)\,\mathrm{wcol}_{5q(t)}(G)\log^{(a)} n$ outliers in terms of weak coloring numbers (Theorem 4), improving the prior bound of $q(t \log n)$ outliers and replacing the $\log n$ dependence by any fixed iterated logarithm.

Proves that for every polynomial $p$ and integer $a \geq 0$ there exists a polynomial $q$ such that $G$ has a balanced separator which is $(\rho/t)$-cheap with $\exp(q(t))\log^{(a)} n$ outliers (Theorem 3), and with $q(t)\,\mathrm{wcol}_{5q(t)}(G)\log^{(a)} n$ outliers in terms of weak coloring numbers (Theorem 4), improving the prior bound of $q(t \log n)$ outliers and replacing the $\log n$ dependence by any fixed iterated logarithm.

1812.09752.studies_paper_title: On the Hat Guessing Number of Graphs
1812.09752.paper_contribution: The paper restates the question and notes that a positive answer seems plausible, but does not prove it.

Proves that every bipartite graph $G$ of sufficiently large maximum degree $\Delta$ satisfies $\operatorname{ch}(G)<(\frac{4}{5}-\varepsilon)\frac{\Delta}{\log\Delta}$ for $\varepsilon=10^{-3}$, giving the first upper bound with leading constant strictly less than $1$ and showing that list coloring behaves fundamentally differently for bipartite graphs than for general triangle-free graphs.

1912.11246.studies_paper_title: Graphs with polynomially many minimal separators
1912.11246.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 1.1 in full, establishing as Theorem 1.2 that every (theta, pyramid, prism, turtle)-free graph $G$ has at most $|V(G)|^{18}$ minimal separators, constructible in polynomial time.

1912.02342.studies_paper_title: Degeneracy of $P_t$-free and $C_{\geq t}$-free graphs with no large complete bipartite subgraphs
1912.02342.paper_contribution: Presents Theorem 4 (degeneracy of $C_{\geq t}$-free graphs excluding $K_{\ell,\ell}$) as the $C_{\geq t}$-free analogue of the Fox–Sudakov biclique version of the conjecture, drawing an explicit structural parallel between the two settings.

1912.02342.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension
1912.02342.paper_contribution: Fully confirms the conjecture, proving that every graph $G$ of VC-dimension at most $d$ contains a clique or stable set of size at least $|G|^{c_d}$ for an explicit $c_d>0$ satisfying $c_d \geq 2^{-2^{O(d)}}$.

1912.02342.studies_paper_title: An algorithmic weakening of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture
1912.02342.paper_contribution: Proves that a constructive version of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture implies the improved approximation conjecture (Conjecture 5), and shows the improved approximation property is closed under graph substitution, establishing Conjecture 5 for many graphs $H$ (e.g., $P_6$-free, fork-free) where the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture remains open.

1912.02342.studies_paper_title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures
1912.02342.paper_contribution: Proves that the polynomial Nikiforov conjecture is equivalent to the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture, establishing all three conjectures as mutually equivalent.

1912.02342.studies_paper_title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures
1912.02342.paper_contribution: Proves that the polynomial Rödl conjecture is equivalent to the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture; as a corollary deduces that the family of string graphs satisfies the polynomial Rödl conjecture.

1912.02342.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the Erdős-Hajnal property
1912.02342.paper_contribution: The paper's results establishing the Erdős-Hajnal property for new (infinitely many prime) graph classes implicitly verify Conjecture 1.7 for those classes, via the equivalence proved by Bucić, Fox, and Pham.

1805.06848.studies_paper_title: A Completion of the Proof of the Edge-statistics Conjecture
1805.06848.paper_contribution: Completes the proof of the conjecture by handling the remaining cases $1 \leq \ell \leq C \cdot k$ (Theorem 1.2), together with the earlier result of Kwan, Sudakov and Tran which covered $C \cdot k \leq \ell \leq \binom{k}{2}/2$.

1805.06848.studies_paper_title: A Completion of the Proof of the Edge-statistics Conjecture
1805.06848.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the sublinear range $1 \leq \ell < k/r$ via Theorem 1.4, establishing $\mathrm{ind}_r(k,\ell) \leq \mathrm{ind}_{\leq r}(k,\ell) \leq \frac{k}{k-r\ell} \cdot \frac{1}{e}$, which yields $e^{-1}+o_k(1)$ when $r\ell = o(k)$; further quantitative bounds for the range $1 \leq \ell \leq (1-\varepsilon)k/r$ are given in Theorem 1.5.

1701.05597.studies_paper_title: Some results and problems on tournament structure
1701.05597.paper_contribution: Proves two partial results in its favour: Theorem 1.3 establishes the case where $A$ is a cyclic triangle (and $\chi(B)\geq c$), and Theorem 1.4 establishes the conjecture for all tournaments with sufficiently large domination number.

1701.05597.studies_paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
1701.05597.paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by constructing, for all integers $\ell, m \geq 1$, a graph $G$ and sets $S, T \subseteq V(G)$ witnessing its failure when $c = k = 3$ (Theorem 1.3); a variant of the counterexample has only one vertex of degree more than three.

1907.12999.paper_contribution: Proves a weak hypergraph extension: for every $t\geq 1$ there exists a finite integer $h(t)$ such that every hypergraph with no $K_t$-minor is $h(t)$-colorable, and establishes $\left\lceil\frac{3}{2}(t-1)\right\rceil\leq h(t)\leq 2g(t)$ where $g(t)$ is the maximum chromatic number of $K_t$-minor-free graphs.

1705.04609.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. VIII. Long odd holes
1705.04609.paper_contribution: The authors note they can prove a weaker partial result — that there exist two holes both of length at least $\ell$ with consecutive lengths — by a refinement of the paper's methods, but omit the details.

1705.04609.studies_paper_title: Dense minors of graphs with independence number two
1705.04609.paper_contribution: Proves that every graph $G$ with $\alpha(G) \leq 2$ contains a minor $H$ with $|V(H)| = \lceil|V(G)|/2\rceil$ and at least $(\gamma - o(1))\binom{|V(H)|}{2}$ edges where $\gamma = 0.986882\ldots$, establishing a dense minor of the exact size predicted by the conjecture for this case (fewer than $1/76$ of all possible edges missing), as progress toward the complete minor.

Proves the conjecture for graphs with chromatic number $k$ and at most $(1.64-\varepsilon)k$ vertices (for any $\varepsilon>0$ and $k$ sufficiently large), and unconditionally for graphs with fewer than $1.4k-0.6$ vertices.

1705.04609.studies_paper_title: Erdős--Pósa property of cycles that are far apart
1705.04609.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full generality with explicit bounds $f(k)\in\mathcal{O}(k^{18}\operatorname{polylog}k)$ and $g(d)=19d$.
1705.04609.attributed_to: Chudnovsky and Seymour; also Ahn, Gollin, Huynh, and Kwon

1705.04609.studies_paper_title: Some results and problems on tournament structure
1705.04609.paper_contribution: Proves two partial results in its favour: Theorem 1.3 establishes the case where $A$ is a cyclic triangle (and $\chi(B)\geq c$), and Theorem 1.4 establishes the conjecture for all tournaments with sufficiently large domination number.

1612.08698.paper_contribution: Proves part (b) in the affirmative: there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every triangle-free planar graph with an assignment of lists of size four is weighted $\varepsilon$-flexible.

1612.08698.studies_paper_title: Flexibility of planar graphs of girth at least six
1612.08698.paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 2: there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every planar graph of girth at least six with lists of size three is weighted $\varepsilon$-flexible, resolving part (c) for girth $\geq 6$ and improving the prior threshold of girth $\geq 12$; part (b) was resolved affirmatively in a companion paper by the same authors.

1810.00811.paper_contribution: The paper discusses the conjecture alongside its own Conjecture 4.3 and notes that no inclusion between $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\chi}}$-binding and the strong Erdős-Hajnal property has been proved, while believing the two conjectures are independent.

1808.01605.studies_paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs
1808.01605.paper_contribution: Shows that the most direct extension of Thomassen's conjecture to digraphs (using minimum out-degree) is false; proves all orientations of $C_3$ and $C_5$ are avoidable; and fully characterizes which digraphs are avoidable when restricted to regular host digraphs.

1808.01605.studies_paper_title: Strengthening Hadwiger's conjecture for $4$- and $5$-chromatic graphs
1808.01605.paper_contribution: Proves the $t = 5$ case as a corollary (Corollary 1.4) of establishing Holroyd's Strong Hadwiger's conjecture for $t = 4$; additionally shows that every 5-chromatic graph contains a $K_5$-minor with a singleton branch-set, and that in a 5-vertex-critical graph any specified vertex may serve as that singleton branch-set.

1808.01605.studies_paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs
1808.01605.paper_contribution: Shows that the most direct extension of Thomassen's conjecture to digraphs (using minimum out-degree) is false; proves all orientations of $C_3$ and $C_5$ are avoidable; and fully characterizes which digraphs are avoidable when restricted to regular host digraphs.

1808.01605.studies_paper_title: Clique minors in graphs with a forbidden subgraph
1808.01605.paper_contribution: Proves that any $K_s$-free $n$-vertex graph with $\alpha(G) \leq r$ has a clique minor of order at least $(n/r)^{1+\frac{1}{10(s-2)}}$ (Theorem 1), which substantially exceeds the bound $n/\alpha(G)$ that Hadwiger's conjecture would imply for those graphs.

1808.01605.studies_paper_title: Fractional chromatic number vs. Hall ratio
1808.01605.paper_contribution: Affirmatively solves the problem by constructing graphs with bounded Hall ratio, arbitrarily large fractional chromatic number, and $\alpha_{\mathrm{deg}_H}(H)\geq |E(H)|/c$ for all subgraphs $H$ (Theorem 1.5).
1808.01605.attributed_to: Dvořák, Ossona de Mendez, and Wu

1708.02370.studies_paper_title: Clustered colouring of graph classes with bounded treedepth or pathwidth
1708.02370.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 4 for all minor-closed classes with bounded pathwidth by establishing $\chi^\star(\mathcal{G}) \leq 2\,\mathrm{tcn}(\mathcal{G}) - 2$ in that setting (Theorem 2), and shows this bound is best possible; the general conjecture remains open.

1708.02370.studies_paper_title: Clustered colouring of graph classes with bounded treedepth or pathwidth
1708.02370.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 3 for all minor-closed classes with bounded treedepth by establishing $\chi_{\Delta}(\mathcal{G}) = \chi^\star(\mathcal{G}) = \mathrm{tcn}(\mathcal{G}) - 1$ in that setting (Theorem 1); the general conjecture remains open.

1708.02370.studies_paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
1708.02370.paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by constructing, for all integers $\ell, m \geq 1$, a graph $G$ and sets $S, T \subseteq V(G)$ witnessing its failure when $c = k = 3$ (Theorem 1.3); a variant of the counterexample has only one vertex of degree more than three.

1710.06282.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions II. Toward walls and their line graphs in graphs of bounded degree
1710.06282.paper_contribution: Proves three theorems supporting the conjecture: (1) every $S_{t,t,t}$-free graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and sufficiently large treewidth contains the line graph of a subdivision of $W_{k\times k}$ as an induced subgraph (Theorem 1.8); (2) every $(t$-theta, $t$-pyramid)-free graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and sufficiently large treewidth contains the line graph of a subdivision of $W_{k\times k}$ as an induced subgraph (Theorem 1.10); (3) for every subcubic subdivided caterpillar $T$, every graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ and sufficiently large treewidth contains a subdivision of $T$ or the line graph of a subdivision of $T$ as an induced subgraph (Theorem 1.11).
1710.06282.attributed_to: Aboulker, Adler, Kim, Sintiari and Trotignon

1710.06282.paper_contribution: Shows this problem is equivalent to Problem 1.2 (Gyárfás's question on $\chi$-boundedness of $\mathcal{C}\overset{\mathfrak{g}}{\cap}\mathcal{C}$), and notes that Felsner et al. answered it in the negative.
1710.06282.attributed_to: Dujmovic, Joret, Morin, Norin, and Wood

1803.03588.studies_paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal
1803.03588.paper_contribution: Improves the exponent to $\delta = 2^{-c(\log(1/x))^2/\log\log(1/x)}$ via Theorem 1.8, a loglog improvement over the Fox–Sudakov bound, but does not achieve the conjectured polynomial bound.

1803.01051.studies_paper_title: A note on connected greedy edge colouring
1803.01051.paper_contribution: Proves the special case $\chi'_c(G) \leq 4$ when $G$ has maximum degree at most 3 (i.e., $\Delta + 1 = 4$), via a reachability lemma for Kempe chains (Theorem 3 and Lemma 7).

1509.06563.studies_paper_title: Some results and problems on tournament structure
1509.06563.paper_contribution: Proves two partial results in its favour: Theorem 1.3 establishes the case where $A$ is a cyclic triangle (and $\chi(B)\geq c$), and Theorem 1.4 establishes the conjecture for all tournaments with sufficiently large domination number.

1701.03366.studies_paper_title: Sparse graphs with bounded induced cycle packing number have logarithmic treewidth
1701.03366.paper_contribution: Theorem 1.1 of this paper (logarithmic feedback vertex set for sparse $\mathcal{O}_k$-free graphs) was the key tool used by Nguyen, Scott, and Seymour [NSS24] to resolve this conjecture in the more general $\mathcal{O}_k$-free case.

1302.2158.studies_paper_title: Planar Graphs of Girth at least Five are Square $(Δ+ 2)$-Choosable
1302.2158.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full and strengthens it to list-coloring and paintability: every planar graph of girth at least 5 with $\Delta(G) \geq \Delta_0 = 2{,}992{,}901$ is square $(\Delta(G)+2)$-choosable and square $(\Delta(G)+2)$-paintable.

1302.2158.studies_paper_title: Fractional coloring of planar graphs of girth five
1302.2158.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 2 for planar graphs of girth at least five with maximum degree at most $\Delta$: such graphs are $(6M_\Delta : 2M_\Delta+1)$-colorable and thus have fractional chromatic number at most $3 - \frac{3}{2M_\Delta+1}$.

1302.2158.studies_paper_title: (3a:a)-list-colorability of embedded graphs of girth at least five
1302.2158.paper_contribution: Proves that the class of minimal obstructions of girth at least five to list $(3a:a)$-colorability is strongly hyperbolic (Theorem 5), which is stated as a key step in a forthcoming proof that the conjecture holds for planar graphs of girth at least five with bounded maximum degree.

1702.01607.paper_contribution: The paper introduces Conjecture 5.3 (large dom implies an $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-cluster) as an intermediate conjecture between Theorem 5.1 and Conjecture 5.2, and establishes implications among the three statements.
The paper notes that proving $S_k$ is a rebel for every $k$ would imply Conjecture 5.3, thereby connecting this conjecture to the clique-number framework developed in the paper.
1702.01607.attributed_to: Chudnovsky, Kim, Liu, Seymour and Thomassé

1702.01607.paper_contribution: The paper introduces Conjecture 5.3 (large dom implies an $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-cluster) as an intermediate conjecture between Theorem 5.1 and Conjecture 5.2, and establishes implications among the three statements.

1611.03196.paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture by showing $\operatorname{inv}(n) = (1+o(1))n$; specifically, $\operatorname{inv}(n) \geq n - \sqrt{2n\log n}$ for sufficiently large $n$ via a random construction (Theorem 8), far exceeding $\lfloor(n-1)/2\rfloor$.

1610.00876.studies_paper_title: Oriented cycles in digraphs of large outdegree
1610.00876.paper_contribution: Resolves the second question in the negative by proving that neither $\overleftrightarrow{K_4}$ nor $\overleftrightarrow{S_4}$ is $\kappa'$-maderian (Propositions 10 and 11); the $\kappa$-maderian question remains open.
1610.00876.attributed_to: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé

1610.00876.studies_paper_title: Oriented cycles in digraphs of large outdegree
1610.00876.paper_contribution: No direct progress on this conjecture is made in this paper; the paper instead proves the related Conjecture 5 concerning oriented cycles.
1610.00876.attributed_to: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé

1610.00876.studies_paper_title: Oriented cycles in digraphs of large outdegree
1610.00876.paper_contribution: Proves this conjecture in full generality (Theorem 6), establishing that $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}$ of an oriented cycle of length $\ell$ is bounded by a polynomial function $K(\ell)$.
1610.00876.attributed_to: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé

1610.00876.paper_contribution: The paper fully resolves the conjecture: Theorem 7 proves $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(F)=v(F)$ for every octus digraph $F$, and Corollary 8 specialises this to every orientation of a cactus graph, which in particular covers all orientations of cycles.
1610.00876.attributed_to: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé

1610.00876.paper_contribution: The paper fully resolves the conjecture: Theorem 7 proves $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(F)=v(F)$ for every octus digraph $F$, and Corollary 8 specialises this to every orientation of a cactus graph, which in particular covers all orientations of cycles.
1610.00876.attributed_to: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé

1610.00876.paper_contribution: Paper recalls the conjecture as broader context without making direct progress on it.

1610.00876.studies_paper_title: Oriented cycles in digraphs of large outdegree
1610.00876.paper_contribution: Improves the upper bound to $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(C(k_1,k_2)) \leq k_1 + 3k_2 - 5$ for $k_1 \geq k_2 \geq 2$ (Theorem 8); shows the bound is sharp for $k_2=2$, giving $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(C(k,2)) = k+1$ for every $k \geq 1$.
1610.00876.attributed_to: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé

1609.06257.studies_paper_title: Gallai's path decomposition in planar graphs
1609.06257.paper_contribution: Answers the question positively for planar graphs (Theorem 1.2): every connected planar graph on $n$ vertices, except $K_3$ and $K_5^-$ (the only two planar odd semi-cliques), can be decomposed into $\lfloor n/2 \rfloor$ paths.

1608.03040.paper_contribution: Answers the question negatively by proving that deciding whether a given digraph is majority 2-colorable is NP-complete (Theorem 9).
1608.03040.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood

1608.03040.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for digraphs with chromatic number at most 6 or dichromatic number at most 3; proves the stronger majority 3-choosability for digraphs with $\Delta^+(D) \leq 4$ or $\Delta(D) \leq 7$, which covers 3-regular and 4-regular digraphs.
1608.03040.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood

1608.03040.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for digraphs with chromatic number at most 6 or dichromatic number at most 3; proves the stronger majority 3-choosability for digraphs with $\Delta^+(D) \leq 4$ or $\Delta(D) \leq 7$, which covers 3-regular and 4-regular digraphs.
1608.03040.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood

1608.03040.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for digraphs with chromatic number at most 6 or dichromatic number at most 3; proves the stronger majority 3-choosability for digraphs with $\Delta^+(D) \leq 4$ or $\Delta(D) \leq 7$, which covers 3-regular and 4-regular digraphs.
1608.03040.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood

1608.03040.paper_contribution: Shows $K \leq 3.9602$ (Theorem 6) via a probabilistic coloring with deterministic alteration; also shows that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^+(D) \geq C(1/\varepsilon)^2 \ln(2/\varepsilon)$ has a fractional majority $(2+\varepsilon)$-coloring (Theorem 7).
1608.03040.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood

1608.03040.paper_contribution: Shows $K \leq 3.9602$ (Theorem 6) via a probabilistic coloring with deterministic alteration; also shows that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^+(D) \geq C(1/\varepsilon)^2 \ln(2/\varepsilon)$ has a fractional majority $(2+\varepsilon)$-coloring (Theorem 7).
1608.03040.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood

1608.03040.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for digraphs with chromatic number at most 6 or dichromatic number at most 3; proves the stronger majority 3-choosability for digraphs with $\Delta^+(D) \leq 4$ or $\Delta(D) \leq 7$, which covers 3-regular and 4-regular digraphs.
1608.03040.attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood

1608.03040.paper_contribution: The paper notes that proving $S_k$ is a rebel for every $k$ would imply Conjecture 5.3, thereby connecting this conjecture to the clique-number framework developed in the paper.
1608.03040.attributed_to: Chudnovsky, Kim, Liu, Seymour and Thomassé

1507.08208.paper_contribution: The paper disproves Conjecture 3 for trees of maximum degree three by constructing a counterexample based on complete binary trees $T_k$ of arbitrary depth $k$, showing that no function $f$ can make the conjecture hold in that case.

Proves the path case with optimal 3-edge-connectivity (replacing the 24-edge-connectivity of [3]), establishing that $f(2) = 3$ is achievable; notes the general conjecture is false for trees of maximum degree three.

1605.07411.studies_paper_title: (P6, triangle)-free digraphs have bounded dichromatic number
1605.07411.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the special case of $\overrightarrow{P}_6$-free digraphs with $\omega(D) \leq 2$ (triangle-free), establishing $\overrightarrow{\chi}(D) \leq 382$ (Theorem 1.3).
Paper recalls the conjecture as broader context without making direct progress on it.

1605.07411.studies_paper_title: Decomposing and colouring some locally semicomplete digraphs
1605.07411.paper_contribution: Proves the first open special case $F = S_2^+$, $H = C_3$: every locally out-transitive oriented graph has dichromatic number at most 2 (Theorem 3.2), and in fact proves the stronger Theorem 3.3 used for induction.

1602.05184.studies_paper_title: Cops and Robber on Hyperbolic Manifolds
1602.05184.paper_contribution: No direct contribution to Meyniel's Conjecture; cited as motivation — if Conjecture 1.3 (which Conjecture 1.5 implies) were established, it would yield the Weak Meyniel's Conjecture.
